reports.title: Ref ruins the final day as Stags end season with heavy defeat against Dar United
reports.summary: A tough way to end what has been a brilliant first season. The Stags played some decent stuff on the ball but found themselves 2-0 down at the break, with the referee making his presence felt throughout. Stan O'Toole was then harshly sent off for a soft foul on the keeper — and that sparked the free fall. The goals kept coming, and Logan McDonald followed him down the tunnel later on, along with one of Dar United's men, as the game completely fell apart. The scoreline is hard to take but it doesn't define what's been a fantastic debut campaign in the Middlesex County Sunday League. Heads high. 🦌

reports.title: Stags battle hard for a point against stubborn Ryefield
reports.summary: Another tough battle against a good side. The Stags started brilliantly — Garnham curled in a stunning freekick to open the scoring before adding a second to make it 2-0. Garnham thought he had his hat-trick soon after but the effort was ruled offside. Ryefield clawed one back with a well-taken header from a set-piece. A nasty challenge on Carmody forced him off, with Harper stepping up between the sticks to help the Stags see out the half. The Stags pushed hard in the second half but a cruel bobble just as Harper was about to clear saw it bounce over his foot and into the net to level things up. Few chances either side after that, and the game ended with Ryefield's keeper seeing red. The Stags can be proud of how hard they battled for the full ninety minutes. 🦌

reports.title: Stags fall to Kensington at the top of the table
reports.summary: A tough afternoon away to the league leaders. The Stags battled hard but Kensington's quality showed in front of goal. M.Wood was named Man of the Match for a commanding display at the back, putting his body on the line to limit the damage. The lads bounce back next week against Ryefield.

reports.title: Stags edge thriller against Samurai
reports.summary: A proper end-to-end Sunday League scrap. Goals from Chahal, Jones, Richards and Z.Rogers (MOTM) saw the Stags hold on for a vital three points. Top performance.

reports.title: Richards seals it at Hounslow
reports.summary: Away day grit. O'Toole opened the scoring before the home side levelled, but Richards' second-half strike won it. Norman bagged the MOTM award for an influential performance from midfield, getting up and down the pitch all afternoon.

reports.title: Narrow defeat away at Old Southall
reports.summary: A difficult away day as Old Southall nicked it with a single goal. The Stags created chances but couldn't find the breakthrough. Norman worked tirelessly throughout and was named MOTM.

reports.title: Chahal earns a share of the spoils against Maida Villa
reports.summary: A competitive Sunday afternoon at home against a strong Maida Villa side. Chahal found the net to level it up but neither side could find a winner. Carmody marshalled things well throughout and was named MOTM.

reports.title: Garnham and Watkins fire Stags to away win at Jugg Boyz
reports.summary: Back-to-back wins and another away three points. Garnham and Watkins were on the scoresheet as the Stags showed real quality going forward. Henry ran the game from midfield and was a deserved MOTM.

reports.title: O'Toole earns a point in a tight home draw with Kodak
reports.summary: A frustrating share of the spoils at home. O'Toole's goal looked to have won it but Kodak grabbed a late equaliser. Richards was the standout on the day with a dominant midfield display.

reports.title: Cup run ends at Ryefield in the semi-final
reports.summary: A tough exit from the Patrons Cup. Ryefield were the better side on the day and the Stags couldn't find a way back into it. Bourke scored the consolation and was immense throughout in a difficult afternoon — a well-deserved MOTM.

reports.title: Pusey earns a point as Stags draw away at Chiswick
reports.summary: A gritty point away from home. Pusey scored to give the Stags the lead but Chiswick found an equaliser. Chahal was sharp throughout and earned the MOTM award.

reports.title: Garnham and O'Toole seal narrow home win over Jugg Boyz
reports.summary: The Stags made it count at home with a battling win. Garnham and O'Toole grabbed the goals as the Stags ground out three important points. McDonald was excellent in midfield and earned himself MOTM.

reports.title: Brilliant away performance as Stags put four past Samurai
reports.summary: Outstanding away display in the away kit. Chahal, O'Toole, Rogers and Watkins all got on the scoresheet as the Stags ran out convincing winners. Z. Rogers put in a player-of-the-match performance from the back.

reports.title: Stags through to the semis on penalties in cup thriller against Moor Mead
reports.result: Hillingdon Stags 1–1 Moor Mead (Won on penalties)
reports.summary: What a cup tie. Wood equalised to force the shootout and Carmody was the hero in goal, pulling off crucial saves to send the Stags through to the semi-final. An absolute scenes at the end. Carmody took a well-deserved MOTM.

reports.title: Ryefield too strong as Stags return from the winter break
reports.summary: The Stags returned from the winter break and faced a Ryefield side in blistering form. Norman got one back but the visitors were clinical all afternoon. Harper was bright throughout and earned the MOTM in a difficult game.

reports.title: Big win over Dragons as Stags put down a marker
reports.summary: A proper test against one of the top sides in the division and the Stags delivered. Garnham, Jones (2) and McDonald all scored in a hard-fought home win. Carmody was commanding throughout.

reports.title: Garnham bags four as Stags run riot against Hounslow
reports.summary: Jack Garnham was unplayable on the day, bagging four goals in an emphatic home win. Richards added the fifth. A statement display from the Stags as the season heads into December.

reports.title: Stags into the cup quarters with a convincing win
reports.summary: A controlled cup performance at home. Bourke, McDonald and Richards all scored as the Stags progressed comfortably. Thompson put in a standout display to earn the MOTM.

reports.title: Stags fall narrowly at Dar United in a tight contest
reports.summary: A close one that went the wrong way. Garnham and Murphy both scored but Dar United nicked it with a third late on. Murphy was excellent throughout and rightly took the MOTM.

reports.title: Stags pegged back twice in frustrating home draw
reports.summary: Two goals to the good and the Stags couldn't hold on. Garnham and Pusey both scored but Chiswick worked hard to level it up. Pusey took home MOTM despite the result.

reports.title: Stags progress in the cup as Maida Villa default
reports.summary: The Stags progress to the Patrons Division 4 Challenge Cup quarter-final without kicking a ball. Maida Villa were unable to fulfil the fixture and the tie was awarded to Hillingdon. On to the quarters.

reports.title: Stags bounce back with four away from home
reports.result: Grove Park Rangers II 0–4 Hillingdon Stags
reports.summary: Back to winning ways and then some. Garnham bagged a brace with Pusey and Richards also on the scoresheet in a commanding away performance. Rogers put in a solid shift at the back to earn MOTM.

reports.title: Cup exit as West Drayton prove too strong
reports.summary: The Stags bowed out of the Roger Perry Junior Open Cup in round two. Garnham pulled one back but West Drayton's quality showed throughout. Burlikowski earned MOTM for his efforts between the sticks.

reports.title: Tough afternoon at Maida Villa
reports.summary: A rough one. Maida Villa were clinical on the day and the Stags couldn't find a way back into it after a difficult first half. Bourke grabbed the consolation. Heads up and move on.

reports.title: Four goals and a clean sheet as Stags dismantle Old Southall
reports.summary: A clinical Sunday morning from the Stags. Bourke, Carmody and O'Toole (with a brace) did the damage as Hillingdon kept it tight at the back. A proper team performance all round.

reports.title: Battling point at Kodak after end-to-end draw
reports.summary: The Stags went toe-to-toe with Kodak in a feisty away draw. Garnham, Norman and Thompson all found the net but the hosts kept pegging them back. A point away from home, though the lads will know they had more in the tank.

reports.title: Stags open with a statement — five goals in a dominant home display
reports.result: Hillingdon Stags 5–2 Grove Park Rangers II
reports.summary: The perfect start to the season. Five different scorers — Bourke, Garnham, Henry, Richards and Thompson — as the Stags ran out comfortable winners at home. A real statement of intent from the lads.
